title: WOS Correspondence Metadata Config
description: A WOS Correspondence Metadata Config sidecar document. Declares the metadata schema for correspondence entries stored in case state. Government workflows track correspondence (letters, phone calls, emails, portal submissions, in-person interactions) as part of the case record. The kernel's existing event mechanism handles correspondence events -- events that match no transition are recorded in provenance (Kernel S4.9). This sidecar defines structured metadata for consistent cataloging and retrieval without adding new event types or modifying lifecycle semantics.

$wosCorrespondenceMetadata.description: WOS Correspondence Metadata Config specification version. MUST be '1.0'.

x-lm.intent: Document type marker for WOS Correspondence Metadata Config sidecar

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema URI for editor validation.

targetWorkflow.description: URI of the WOS Kernel Document this correspondence config applies to.

x-lm.intent: Binds this sidecar to its parent kernel workflow

version.description: Version of this Correspondence Metadata Config document.

title.description: Human-readable name for this correspondence configuration.

description.description: Human-readable description of this correspondence configuration.

correspondenceField.description: Case state field path where correspondence entries are stored. The workflow stores an array of correspondence entry objects at this path.

x-lm.intent: Identifies which case state field holds the array of correspondence entries

entryTemplates.description: Templates defining the metadata structure for correspondence entries. Each template declares the channel, direction, actor type, and required fields for a category of correspondence.

examples.description: Physical mail received from applicant or representative

x-lm.intent: Templates that define what metadata each category of correspondence entry must carry

extensions.description: Extension data. All keys MUST be prefixed with 'x-'.

id.description: Unique template identifier. Referenced by correspondence entries via templateRef.

description.description: Human-readable description of when this template applies.
examples: Physical mail received from applicant or representative
Phone call with applicant or representative

channel.description: Communication channel. 'in-person': face-to-face contact. 'phone': telephone call. 'mail': physical mail. 'email': electronic mail. 'portal': web portal submission or message. 'fax': facsimile transmission. Extensible via x- prefixed values in the extensions property.

x-lm.intent: Declares the communication channel for this correspondence category

direction.description: Direction of the correspondence. 'inbound': received by the agency. 'outbound': sent by the agency.

actorType.description: Who sent or received the correspondence. 'applicant': the primary case subject. 'representative': authorized representative acting on behalf of the applicant. 'third-party': external party (e.g., employer, medical provider). 'system': automated system-generated correspondence. 'agency': agency staff.

x-lm.intent: Identifies the actor role for this correspondence category

requiredFields.description: Fields that MUST be present in a correspondence entry using this template, beyond the base metadata (templateRef, channel, direction, actorType, contentRef, summary, timestamp).

extensions.description: Extension data. All keys MUST be prefixed with 'x-'.

templateRef.description: The id of the EntryTemplate this entry follows.

actorType.description: Actor type for this correspondence entry.

contentRef.description: Claim check reference -- a URI or storage reference for the actual content. The content itself is not stored in case state; this is a pointer to it.

x-lm.intent: Pointer to the actual correspondence content stored outside case state

examples: Adverse decision notice mailed to applicant

relatedTaskRef.description: Optional reference to a task this correspondence relates to.
